name: mimir-recall-mini
description: A small, paraphrase-heavy personal-assistant recall set (LOCOMO/LongMemEval-style). Each query is worded differently from the memory that answers it, so keyword-only search is stressed and semantic retrieval is rewarded. Distractors share a domain but not the answer.

memories.note: I drink a strong espresso every morning before work.
I usually bike to the office on weekdays.
My favorite programming language is Rust.
I have a golden retriever named Max.
I'm allergic to peanuts and shellfish.
My wedding anniversary is on June 14th.
I go to the climbing gym on Tuesday and Thursday evenings.
I'm currently reading Dune by Frank Herbert.
I drive a blue 2019 Subaru Outback.
My sister Elena lives in Barcelona.
I work as a backend engineer at a fintech startup.
I play the acoustic guitar and love folk music.
I've been vegetarian for the past five years.
I visited Japan last spring and loved Kyoto.
I switched to an Android phone last month.
The project proposal is due at the end of the quarter.
I use a hardware security key for two-factor authentication.
I'm trying to keep a fiddle-leaf fig alive in my living room.
I grew up in a small town in Vermont.
I'm a lifelong fan of the Boston Celtics.
My dentist appointment is scheduled for next Friday.
I make sourdough bread from scratch on weekends.
I'm afraid of heights, especially glass elevators.
I'm learning Italian using a flashcard app.
